original: French and Ottoman fleets occupy Nice
A polar bear attacks and kills a member of the Second Dutch Expedition to the Arctic on Stateneiland in northern Russia
Spanish silver fleet disappears off Florida Keys; thousands die
Puritans from Massachusetts Bay Colony land at Salem
Siege of Candia on Crete ends with the Venetian fortress surrendering to the Ottomans, the second-longest siege in history (began 1648)
William of Orange's troops reconquer Naarden from France
Swedish Admiral Stenbock sails out with a fleet of 66 ships
Le Plecta is appointed French Minister of Finance
Austrian armies occupy Belgrade
King Wiliam III escapes back to England
First lighthouse is built in North America (Boston)
VOC dismisses Dutch East Indies Governor-General Diederik Durven
David Bushnell's "Turtle" attacks British ship "Eagle" in New York Bay in the first (failed) submarine attack
Hurricane hits Guadeloupe, killing more than 6,000
Hurricane hits Martinique; 100 French and Dutch ships sink and 600 people die
The Battle of Groton Heights takes place, resulting in a British victory
American inventor Thomas Blanchard patents lathe
Oberlin Collegiate Institute in Ohio goes co-ed (4 women, 30 men)
Great fire in New York
National Black Convention meets in Cleveland
After a 59-day siege, Confederate troops vacate Fort Wagner, SC, resulting in 1,700 casualties
First westbound train arrives in San Francisco
Mine fire kills 110 at Avondale, Pennsylvania
Ship sinks in Bay of Biscay, killing 483
Regular cable car service begins on Clay Street, San Francisco
Race riot in Charleston, South Carolina
Southern Pacific railroad line from Los Angeles to San Francisco is completed in California
Chicago White Stockings beat Detroit Wolverines 26-6 at Lake Front Park, Chicago, setting an MLB record with 18 runs in the 7th inning
Cubs' Burns (extra bases), Williamson, and Pfeiffer get three hits in one inning
Eastern Rumelia declares its union with Bulgaria, accomplishing the Unification of Bulgaria
Charles Turner becomes the first bowler to take 250 wickets in an English season
Queen Victoriagrants William Mackinnon's Imperial British East Africa Company political and commercial rights
King Kabaka Mwanga of Buganda abdicates
Carnation processes its first can of evaporated milk
British General Buller occupies Lydenburg, South Africa
Atlanta Life Insurance Company is formed
Chicago White Sox pitcher Frank Smith no-hits the Detroit Tigers, 15-0
General Trade Journal publishes the first Dutch photo of a train accident
Saskatchewan (then Regina) Roughriders football club is formed
NY Giant Jeff Tesreau no-hits Philadelphia, 3-0
Hamilton Alerts apply for ORFU reinstatement, taking the name Hamilton Rowing Club
Jerry Travers wins the 19th US Amateur Golf Championship
French pilot Georges Guynemer shoots down his 54th German aircraft
Charles Paddock wins the 100 and 200 yards at the AAU national senior outdoor track and field championships
Brooklyn Dodgers beat Phillies 22-8
Third Women's World Games (track and field competition for women) take place over three days at the Letná Stadium in Prague, Czechoslovakia
Spanish Civil War: The start of the Battle of El Mazuco
QueenWilhelmina of the Netherlandscelebrates her 40th anniversary
First German air attack on Great Britain in WW II
South Africa declares war on Nazi Germany
World War II: The Battle of Barking Creek
Crown Prince Michael succeeds Carol II as King of Romania
Former French Commander-in-Chief Army General Maurice Gamelin and other military leaders are arrested for their malfeasance
All Jews over age 6 in German territories are ordered to wear a star
Great and Small Vilnius Jewish Ghettos established in Vilna, Lithuania, under Nazi occupation
Czech marathon runner Oskar Hêks is transported to Auschwitz-Birkenau
"Congressional Limited" train derails near Frankford, Philadelphia, killing 79
Carl Scheib becomes the youngest pitcher in American League history at 16 years and 8 months
Gen. von Zangen's 15th Army escapes from Zeeland
A's catcher Greek George punches umpire Joe Rue and gets suspended
All-America Football Conference begins regular season play as the Cleveland Browns beat the Miami Seahawks 44-0 before 60,135 at Cleveland's Municipal Stadium
America's first mass killing: Howard Unruh kills 13 neighbors in 12 minutes in Camden, New Jersey
Canadian TV begins in Montreal
Roy Campanellasets a single-season record of 41 home runs by a catcher
Alan Freed leaves Cleveland for NYC to join WINS radio
US plane is shot down above Siberia
Yankees use a record 10 pinch hitters
J. van Tilburg is appointed governor of Suriname
The US performs a nuclear test over the South Atlantic Ocean, the third and final part of Operation Argus to study the Christofilos effect, a theoretical defensive shield to cloud Soviet radar
American Otis Davis runs a world record 44.9 to win the gold medal in the 400 m at the Rome Olympics; German Carl Kaufmann records the same time but is ruled second via photo finish
Australian middle distance runner Herb Elliott breaks his own world record by winning the 1500 m in 3:35.6 at the Rome Olympics
In a famous victory, American decathlete Rafer Johnson scores an Olympic record of 8,392 points to win the gold medal in Rome, beating his rival and friend C. K. Yang of China by 58 points
USSR performs a nuclear test at Kapustin Yar, USSR
US performs a nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Historian Lee Allen says the Cleveland Indians-Washington Senators game is the 100,000th in Major League Baseball history
The Centre for International Industrial Property Studies (CEIPI) is founded at the University of Strasbourg, France
India invades West Pakistan, beginning the Indo-Pakistani War
KLNE TV channel 3 in Lexington, NE (PBS) begins broadcasting
Race riot in Atlanta, Georgia
American rock band "The Doors" open their first European tour with four sold-out shows over two days at London's 2,500-seat Roundhouse
Swaziland gains independence from Britain (National Day)
Palestinians seize three airliners
USSR performs a nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeastern Kazakhstan
Jerry Lewis's sixth Muscular Dystrophy telethon raises $8,125,387
Summer Olympics resume in Munich, Germany, after the massacre of 11 Israeli athletes by the Black September Palestinian terrorist organization
The New York Times reports that almost all Superfectas run at Yonkers, Roosevelt, and Monticello from January to March of 1973 were fixed
Saudi Arabia increases its oil buy-back price from 93 percent to 94.9 percent of the posted price
6.8-magnitude earthquake along Anatolian Fault kills over 2,000 in Lice, Turkey
US performs a nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
"The Robert MacNeil Report" becomes "The MacNeil-Lehrer Report" (currently known as "PBS NewsHour") on PBS
Russian pilot Belenko defects to Japan in a MiG-25 jet
Angels acquireDave Kingmanfrom Padres for cash, 9 days later Yankees buy Kingman (started with Mets) who plays in all 4 divisions in 1977
USSR performs an underground nuclear test
French runner Chantal Langlacé sets the women's 100 km world record in 7:27.22 in Amiens, France
Macalester University of St. Paul, Minnesota, beats Mount Senario 17-14
US College football longest losing streak of 50 games ends for Macalester College (St. Paul)
Robert Granville "Bob" Lemon is named NY Yankees manager for the second time
Dutch Internal Minister Mr. M. Red assigns BVD to spy on communists
Jerry Lewis's 17th Muscular Dystrophy telethon raises $28,400,000
Polish dissidents seize Polish Embassy in Bern, Switzerland
Soviet Union admits to shooting down the South Korean airliner KAL 007 on September 1
Lanford Wilson's "Balm in Gilead" premieres in New York City
Today Show begins live remote broadcasts from Moscow
Mark Messierloses control of his Porsche and totals three cars; he is charged with hit-and-run and careless driving and is ordered to pay a fine
Midwest Express Airlines Flight 105, a Douglas DC-9, crashes just after takeoff from Milwaukee, Wisconsin, killing 31
300 invitees pay $5,000 to hearBarbra Streisand's benefit concert
Attack on synagogue in Istanbul kills 23
Jozef Pribilinec sets a speed walking world record time (15,447 km)
Michael Spinks TKO's Steffen Tangstad in the 4th round for the heavyweight boxing title
USSR charges correspondent Nicholas Daniloff with spying
Conjoined twins Benjamin and Patrick Binder are separated at Johns Hopkins Hospital
Douglas Wakiihuri wins a marathon in 2:11.48
Saskatchewan's Dave Ridgway kicks a CFL-record 60-yard field goal
Crippled Soviet Soyuz TM-5 lands safely with two cosmonauts aboard
Tom Gregory (11) becomes the youngest person to swim the English Channel
USSR performs an underground nuclear test
Amateur Athletic Federation strips Ben Johnson of all track records
Due to an error in the police computer system, 41,000 Parisians are mailed legal notices accusing them of manslaughter and prostitution
A US citizen is shot in Kuwait, and oil markets surge due to aggressive US statements toward Iraq
Ronald Venetiaan is chosen as president of Suriname
The name Saint Petersburg is restored to Russia's second largest city, which had been renamed Leningrad in 1924
USSR recognizes the independence of the three Baltic republics (Latvia, Estonia, and Lithuania)
Noureddine Morceli runs a world record in the 1500 m (3:28.86)
Jerry Lewis's 28th Muscular Dystrophy telethon raises $46,014,922
Franziska van Almsick swims a women's world record in the 200 m freestyle (1:56.78)
Baltimore Orioles'Eddie Murrayhits his 500th career home run
Beaux Arts Trio (Menahem Pressler, piano; Daniel Hope, violin; Antonio Meneses, cello) performs its final concert after 53 years in Lucerne, Switzerland
61 illegal immigrants die after a fishing boat capsizes off the coast of Turkey
Islamist militants kill 20 people in villages in northeastern Nigeria
German police confirm more than 13,000 refugees have arrived in southern Germany in the last two days, fleeing conflicts in Iraq, Syria, and Afghanistan
Minnesota man Danny Heinrich admits to kidnapping and murdering 11-year-old Jacob Wetterling on October 22, 1989
India's Supreme Court legalizes gay sex, overturning a colonial-era law
New York and New Jersey state attorneys launch investigations into sexual abuse by the Catholic clergy, bringing to six the number of states investigating
Remains of 166 people, 2 years old, are found in a mass grave in Veracruz state, Mexico
World Surf League announces that from 2019 there will be equal prize money for men and women across elite tour events, becoming the first US-based global sports league with gender pay parity
Sri Lankan cricket fast bowler Lasith Malinga takes 4 wickets in 4 balls and finishes with 5 for 6 as New Zealand is all out for 88 in a 37-run defeat in the 3rd T-20 International in Pallekele
Los Angeles County records its highest-ever temperature of 121°F (49.4°C)
Premier Daniel Andrews announces a roadmap out of restrictions for the Australian state of Victoria but extends the lockdown until September 28
Strain of bacteria nicknamed "Conan the Bacterium" survives three years attached to the International Space Station in open space
Fire in a karaoke bar near Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam, kills at least 32
Africa's first climate summit opens in Nairobi, Kenya, calling for greater climate-related financing from industrialized countries
American rockerBruce Springsteen(73) postpones the remaining eight shows of his summer tour under doctor's advice to treat peptic ulcer disease
Egypt’s President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi calls for a major reduction in the country's birth rate to avoid a catastrophe, from more than two million to 400,000 a year
Mexico's Supreme Court decriminalizes abortion nationwide, building on an earlier state-by-state ruling
Russian daytime missile attack on a Ukrainian market in Kostyantynivka kills 17 civilians
US sanctions Abdelrahim Hamdan Dagalo, Rapid Support Forces leader, for acts of violence, and human rights abuses, including the massacre of civilians in Darfur
Philadelphia Eagles win 34-29 over Green Bay Packers at Corinthians Arena in São Paulo, Brazil, in the first NFL football game in the Southern Hemisphere
Red Bull's Max Verstappen sets fastest Formula One lap in history with 1:18.792, surpassing Lewis Hamilton's record of 1:18.887, at the Italian Grand Prix
US Open Women's Tennis: Aryna Sabalenka of Belarus becomes first woman since Serena Williams (2013-14) to claim back-to-back US titles with a 6-3, 7-6 win over Amanda Anisimova

Venice Film Festival: "Father Mother Sister Brother," directed by Jim Jarmusch and starring Cate Blanchett and Adam Driver, wins the Golden Lion; "The Voice of Hind Rajab," directed by Kaouther Ben Hania, awarded the Grand Jury prize
Battle of the Frigidus (Frigid River) in Northern Italy is fought in high winds. Eastern Roman EmperorTheodosius Idefeats Western Emperor Eugenius, who is captured and beheaded.
Conrad II, Holy Roman Emperor becomes King of Burgundy, succeeding Rudolf III
Christopher Columbus' fleet leaves Gomera, Canary Islands
Ferdinand Magellan's Spanish expedition, now led byJuan Sebastián Elcano, returns to Seville without its captain after a three-year epic voyage, becoming the first to circumnavigate the globe

Battle of Nördlingen: Imperial-Spanish forces led by Ferdinand of Hungary and Cardinal-Infante Ferdinandheavily defeat a combined Swedish and German Protestant army led by Gustav Horn and Bernhard of Saxe-Weimar
KingCharles IIof England spends a day hiding in an oak tree during his escape after losing the Battle of Worcester
WolfgangMozart's dramatic opera "La Clemenza di Tito" premieres at the Estates Theatre in Prague
GeneralUlysses S. Grantoccupies Paducah, Kentucky
Stonewall Jacksonoccupies Frederick, Maryland
Frederick Douglassis the first Black delegate to a national convention in the US
First Test cricket game in England begins withW. G. Gracescoring 152 on debut against Australia at The Oval
Queen Victoriaestablishes the Distinguished Service Order
Lord Kitchenerdestroys Mahdi's tomb in Omdurman
US Secretary of StateJohn Haypublishes his "Open Door Note" calling for equality of commercial opportunity for foreigners in China
US PresidentWilliam McKinleyis shot and assassinated by anarchist Leon Czolgosz while visiting the Pan-American Exposition in Buffalo, New York
New York Times headline announces American explorerRobert Pearydiscovered the North Pole 5 months earlier (now thought unlikely)
Jack Dempseyknocks out Billy Miske in the third round for the heavyweight boxing title in the first radio broadcast of a prizefight
QueenWilhelmina of the Netherlandscelebrates her silver jubilee
Assassination attempt onBenito Mussolinifails
The Soviet Union signs theKellogg-BriandPact
Democratically elected Argentine presidentHipólito Yrigoyenis deposed in a military coup
"Steamboat Round the Bend," a film directed byJohn Fordand starringWill Rogers, is released weeks after Rogers' death
Terence Rattigan's play "The Winslow Boy" premieres in London
Juliana is crowned Queen of The Netherlands
Konrad Adenaueris re-elected as German Chancellor for a second term, and his party, the CDU, is one seat short of a majority in the German Federal Republic
"La Strada," directed byFederico Felliniand starringAnthony Quinn, premieres at the Venice Film Festival
Elvisrecords "White Christmas," "Silent Night," and "Here Comes Santa Claus"
RockerJerry Lee Lewisleaves Sun Records as his contract expires and signs with Smash Records
Eric Claptonrecords guitar tracks forGeorge Harrison's Beatles song "While My Guitar Gently Weeps"
British Prime MinisterEdward Heathmeets with Irish Prime Minister/TaoiseachJack Lynchat Chequers in England to discuss the situation in Northern Ireland
William Craig andIan Paisleyspeak at a rally in Belfast before a crowd of approximately 20,000 people and call for the establishment of a "third force" to defend Ulster
SingerFrank Sinatrabrings surprise guestDean Martinto his appearance onJerry Lewis's 11th Muscular Dystrophy Association telethon, the first time Martin and Lewis share a stage in 20 years
Parlophone/EMI releasesPaul McCartney's single "Tug of War" from the album of the same name
Pittsburgh Pirates retireWillie Stargell's #8
"Amadeus," from the play byPeter Shaffer, directed by Milos Forman and starring Tom Hulce, premieres in Los Angeles (Academy Award for Best Picture 1985)
Actor Jackson Pinckney is awarded $487,000 for being partially blinded byJean-Claude Van Dammeduring the filming of "Cyborg"
Cal Ripken Jr. plays 2,131 consecutive games, breaking Gehrig's record
US Senate Ethics Committee votes 6-0 to recommend the expulsion of SenatorBob Packwoodover allegations of sexual abuse
Funeral ofDiana, Princess of Wales, is held at Westminster Abbey in London
"New Moon," the second book inStephenie Meyer's "Twilight Saga," is published by Little, Brown and ends up selling 5.3 million copies
Barack Obamaaccepts the Democratic nomination for US President
Catalonia's parliament passes a law to allow a referendum on independence from Spain
Mother Teresais declared co-patron saint of the Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Calcutta alongside St.Francis Xavier
Brazilian presidential candidateJair Bolsonarois stabbed at a campaign rally in Juiz de Fora, Brazil
100,000 people demonstrate in Minsk against Belarusian PresidentAlexander Lukashenkoamid month-long protests against his re-election
First chord change in 7 years inJohn Cage's "As Slow As Possible" composition is being played for 639 years in Halberstadt, Germany
August 11, 3114 BBCE is the widely accepted date as the starting point of the Maya/Mesoamerican Long Count Calendar; this date is the corresponding date on the Julian Calendar
After St. Paul's Cathedral and much of the city burned down over four days, the Great Fire of London is finally extinguished
Cherokee Nation unites and ratifies constitution at Tahlequah, Oklahoma
World War I: First Battle of the Marne begins as French and British forces prevent the German advance on Paris
First true supermarket, "Piggly Wiggly," is established by Clarence Saunders in Memphis, Tennessee; the grand opening is delayed by construction until the 11th
Hurricane Irma makes landfall on the Caribbean islands of Barbuda, Sint Maarten, and the British Virgin Islands. Prime Minister Gaston Browne reports 95% of buildings in Barbuda are damaged
